name: Stucco cob wall
description: Smooth, monolithic wall finish evoking stucco rendering or cob (mud-and-straw) construction. The wall is a single course (thickness=1) of one uniform block — no banding, no exposed framing, no contrasting trim — chosen to read as plastered earth or lime-washed render. Common in Mediterranean villages (whitewashed walls, terraces in the Cyclades, Provence, Andalusia) and in southwestern / adobe / mud-brick vernaculars (pueblo, North African ksar). The finish covers the full wall extent, including around openings, so doors and windows appear cut directly into the mass. Soft, warm tones (sand, white, ochre) and a complete absence of joints are the signature; texture comes from light and shadow on the volume, not from material variation.
